sections.title: Talking to the assistant without any planks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find planks of wood? I need some for the telescope's base.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I understand planks can be found at Port Khazard, to the east of here. There are some at the Barbarian Outpost, too.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Failing that, you could always ask the Sawmill Operator. He's to the north-east of Varrock, by the Lumber Yard.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant with less than three not normal planks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a plank.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: That you do - but if you remember rightly, the professor wanted an ordinary wooden plank, like the ones at Port Khazard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant with three not normal planks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about this plank I've got here?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I think the professor was after ordinary planks. You should be able to find them at Port Khazard.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant with less than three planks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got a plank.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: That's nice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, for the telescope's base.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Well done. Remember that you'll need three in total.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant with three planks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some planks for the telescope's base.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Good work! The professor has been eagerly awaiting them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi again!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: It's my helping hand, back again.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Do you have the planks yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, not yet. Three planks was it?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: It was indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi again!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: It's my helping hand, back again.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Do you have the planks yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made a start.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Well, you'll need three of them.

sections.title: Using a not normal plank on the observatory professor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a plank for you.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: That's no good. I asked for ordinary planks. If you can't find out where they are, talk to my assistant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry.

sections.title: Returning with three not normal planks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi again!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: It's my helping hand, back again.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Do you have the planks yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about this plank?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: I asked for ordinary wooden planks. If you're not sure where to get them, speak to my assistant.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I was...

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Welcome to the magnificent wonder of the Observatory, where wonder is all around you, where the stars can be clutched from the heavens!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, nice intro.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Why, thanks! How might I help you?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Lost? It must have been those pesky goblins that led you astray.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Head north-east to find the city of Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I'll find the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for all your help.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: No problem at all. Come and visit again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An Observatory?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Of course. We have a superb telescope up in the Observatory, on the hill.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: A truly marvellous invention, the likes of which you'll never behold again.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Well, it would be if it worked.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Don't interrupt!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? It doesn't work?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oh, no, no, no. Don't listen to him, he's joking. Aren't you, my FAITHFUL assistant?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Nope, dead serious. Hasn't been working for a long time.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Arghhh! Get back to work and stop sticking your nose in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, it's broken. How come?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oh, I suppose there's no use keeping it secret. Did you see those houses outside?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Up on the hill? Yes, I've seen them.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: It's a horde of goblins.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Since they moved here they have caused nothing but trouble.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Last week, my telescope was tampered with.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Now, parts need replacing before it can be used again. They've even been messing around in the dungeons under this area. Something needs to be done.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Strikes me that this visitor could help us.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Stop being so rude.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Although, he has a point. What do you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds interesting, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oh, thanks so much.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: I shall need some materials for the telescope, so it can be used again.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Let's start with three planks of wood for the telescope base. My assistant will help with obtaining these, won't you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: As if I don't have enough work to do. Seems I don't have a choice.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Go talk to him if you need some advice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be right back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry, I don't have time for that.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oh dear. I really do need some help.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: If you see anyone who can help then please send them my way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just passing through.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Fair enough. Not everyone is interested in this place, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: The traveller returns!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still working hard?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Some of us are.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: What did I tell you about speaking when spoken to?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: So, [player name], you have the bronze bar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Please bring me one, then.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant without a bronze bar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me? How do I go about getting a bronze bar?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: You'll need to use tin and copper ore on a furnace to produce this metal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right you are.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant with a bronze bar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The bronze bar is ready, and waiting for the professor.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: He'll surely be pleased. Go ahead and give it to him.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi again!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: It's my helping hand, back again.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Do you have the planks yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've got them. Here they are.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Well done. This will make a big difference.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Now, the bronze for the tube. Oh, assistant!

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Okay, okay, ask me if you need any help, [player name].

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: How are you getting on finding me some molten glass?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still working on it.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: I really need it. Please hurry.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant without molten glass

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the best way for me to get molten glass?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: There are many ways, but I'd suggest making it yourself. Get yourself a bucket of sand and some soda ash, which you can get from using seaweed with a furnace. Use the soda ash and sand together in a

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: furnace and bang - molten glass is all yours. There's a book about it on the table if you want to know more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you!

sections.title: Talking to the assistant with molten glass

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've managed to get hold of some molten glass.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suggest you have a word with the professor, in that case.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: The traveller returns!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still working hard?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Some of us are.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: What did I tell you about speaking when spoken to?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: So, [player name], you have the bronze bar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I certainly do. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Great. Now all I need is the lens made.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Please get me some molten glass.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oi! Lazy bones!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? I'm not lazy.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Not you! I'm talking to my assistant.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Calm down old man, I heard. [player name], I'm here if you need any help.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Thank you. Wait a minute, who you calling 'old'?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Did you bring me the mould?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still looking for it.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Please try and find it; again, my assistant may be able to help.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant without the lens mould

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this lens mould you mentioned?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I'm sure I heard one of those goblins talking about it. I bet they've hidden it somewhere. Probably using it for some strange purpose, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you say that?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I had a nice new star chart, until recently. I went out to do an errand for the professor the other day, only to see a goblin using it...

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: ...as some kind of makeshift hankey to blow his nose!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: You may want to look through the dungeon they have under their little village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: How are you getting on finding me some molten glass?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here it is.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Excellent work, let's make the lens.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: It'll need to be made to an exact shape and size.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Well, obviously, hence why we have a lens mould.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Not any more. One of those goblins took it.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Great, just what I need. [player name], I don't suppose you could find it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have a look - where should I start?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: No idea. You could ask my USELESS assistant if you want.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: What have I done to deserve this?

sections.title: Searching the key chest with one in the inventory
Speaking to the observatory assistant with the goblin kitchen key

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a key.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Perhaps it unlocks something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, thanks. Great advice.

sections.title: Using the goblin kitchen key on the observatory assistant

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look. I found this.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: A key?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: To what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't know. Perhaps some amazing treasure!

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I doubt it.

sections.title: Using the goblin kitchen key on the observatory professor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I found this!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: It doesn't look much like a lens mould.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? No. It's a key!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: To what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well,I don't really know yet.

sections.title: Talking to the observatory professor with the goblin kitchen key

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Did you bring me the mould?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but I found a key.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Interesting. You might be onto something there. Keep looking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do, thanks.

sections.title: Attempting to unlock the kitchen gate without waking the goblin guard
Subsequent attempts after unlocking to open the kitchen gate without waking the goblin guard

lines.speaker: Goblin guard
lines.text: Oi, how dare you wake me up!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You had better be quick, there may be more guards about.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant with the lens mould

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the lens mould.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Well done on finding that! I am honestly quite impressed. Make sure you take it straight to the professor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Euuuw, that smells awful!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Is the lens finished?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make it again?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Use the molten glass with the mould.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh. Simple.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant without the observatory lens

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How should I make this lens?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Just use the molten glass with the mould. Simple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: How are you getting on finding me some molten glass?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here it is.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Did you bring me the mould?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I certainly have. You'll never guess what they were doing with it.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Well, from the smell I'd guess cooking some vile concoction.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, good guess. Well, here you go.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Please don't give that to him. Last time he tried any Crafting, I had to spend a week cleaning up after the explosion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Explosion?

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Erm, yes. I think in this instance you had probably better do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose it's better I don't ask.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: You can use the mould with molten glass to make a new lens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hold on a second. I don't have space to carry it all.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hurry and free up some space, then.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant with the observatory lens

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interrupt you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: I suppose so. Please be quick though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Might I have a word?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Sure, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak with you?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Why, of course. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you like this lens? Good, huh?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Nice. What's that scratch?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, erm, that's a feature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's it! Indubitably, it facilitates the triangulation of photonic illumination to the correct...

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Stop! You can't confuse me with big words. Just pray the professor doesn't notice.

sections.title: Talking to the assistant before going up to the observatory

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Ah, it's the telescope repairman! The professor is waiting for you in the Observatory.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I get to the Observatory?

lines.speaker: Observatory assistant
lines.text: Well, since the bridge was ruined, you will have to travel through the dungeon under the goblins' settlement.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, here it is. You may as well take this mould too.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Wonderful, at last I can fix the telescope.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Would you accompany me to the Observatory? You simply must see the telescope in operation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds interesting. Count me in.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Superb. You'll have to go via the dungeon under the goblin settlement, seeing as the bridge is broken. You'll find stairs up to the Observatory from there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. See you there.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Welcome back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, this really is impressive.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Certainly is. Please, take a look through the telescope and tell me what you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, professor!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Oh, hi there.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: I'm just adding the finishing touches.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, don't let me interrupt.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Right, let's get this finished.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Hmmmm...

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Bit of a tap here...

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: [player name], I'm just going upstairs to finish off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right-oh.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: In goes the lens.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Looking good.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: And one final adjustment.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: And all our work pays off.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Welcome back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had a look through the telescope.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: What did you see? If you're not sure, you can find out by looking at the star charts dotted around the walls downstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aquarius!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: That's exactly it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! Woo hoo!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: That's Aquarius, the water-bearer.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: It seems suitable, then, to award you with water runes!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: By Saradomin's earlobes! You must be a friend of the gods indeed.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Look in your backpack for your reward, in payment for your work.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Have you got some space for your reward?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Reward? Oh, no, I haven't.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Come see me again when you have a few free spaces in your inventory.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: I'm afraid not. Have another look. Remember, you can check the star charts on the walls for reference.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Capricorn!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: That's exactly it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! Woo hoo!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: That's Capricorn, the goat.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: Capricorn will surely reward your insight with an increase to your Strength.

lines.text: Yes! Woo hoo!

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: That's Sagittarius, the centaur.

lines.speaker: Observatory professor
lines.text: As you've spotted the archer, I shall reward you with a maple longbow.
